title: How coal mining sites respond to extreme rainfall: Deep-learning-enhanced InSAR observations across the 2021 Henan 7.20 extreme rainfall

explanation: 该研究专注于开罗大都市区的空气污染，分析了PM2.5的来源和健康风险，涉及空气质量和大气成分分析，与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测和大气成分分析密切相关。
authors: Eliane Farah;Marc Fadel;Nansi Fakhri;Hassan R. Dhaini;Konstantina Oikonomou;Minas Iakovides;Aliki Christodoulou;Salwa K. Hassan;Mohamed Boraiy;Mostafa El-Nazer;Ali Wheida;Magdy Abdelwahab;Stéphane Sauvage;Agnès Borbon;Jean Sciare;Frédéric Ledoux;Dominique Courcot;Charbel Afif
title: Insights into air pollution at an urban site in the Greater Cairo Megacity: Minor PM<sub>2.5</sub> sources with significant health risks
abstract:
•Source apportionment revealed 11 PM2.5sources in the Greater Cairo Area. •Industrial sources of coal, copper, and lead have a PM2.5share of 12.2%. •Elemental PM2.5in GCA exceeds safety thresholds, with NCR 2.9 and CR 3.9 × 10−5. •Local primary particulate controllable sources dominate PM2.5health risks. •Traffic and industry accounted for 40% elemental-NCR and 37% elemental-CR. 
 This study presents a comprehensive chemical characterization and source apportionment of PM2.5 in the Greater Cairo Area (GCA), one of the world's most polluted megacities. A total of 59 PM2.5 samples were collected continuously and on a 24-h basis during the winter of 2019–2020 at an urban background site and analyzed for a wide range of organic and inorganic species. The Positive Matrix Factorization (PMF) model was applied to identify and quantify as many as eleven sources contributing to PM2.5, highlighting the highly complex mixture of aerosols over the GCA. These sources include industrial emissions (coal combustion, lead and copper smelting), vehicular exhaust and non-exhaust emissions, open waste and wood burning, cooking, processed secondary aerosols, transported crustal dust, and mixed regional pollution. Local primary particulate controllable sources dominated elemental health risks, contributing 60% of the total non-cancer risk (NCR:1.7) and 52% of the total cancer risk (CR:2.1 × 10−5), despite representing a much smaller fraction of PM2.5 mass. Industrial emissions, though contributing only ∼12% of PM2.5 mass, were responsible for 37% of elemental NCR and 29% of CR. These findings underscore the need for targeted mitigation strategies addressing the burden of air pollution in GCA.

explanation: 该论文使用Sentinel-5P卫星观测数据结合地面气象数据，通过机器学习方法估算马德里地区的地表NO2浓度，这与大气环境遥感技术中的空气质量监测密切相关。
authors: Carlos Morillas;Rocco Giosa;Sergio Alvarez;Carmine Serio;Guido Masiello;Giuliano Liuzzi;Carmen Aviles;Sara Martinez
title: Estimating surface-level NO<sub>2</sub> concentrations in the Madrid region using Sentinel-5P observations and ground-based meteorological data with machine learning approaches
abstract:
•Surface NO2estimated by combining Sentinel-5P and routine meteorological data. •Dual temporal aggregation captures overpass and antecedent atmospheric conditions. •Environmentally coherent spatial clustering improves model accuracy. •High performance achieved without emission inventories or CTM outputs. •Framework supports air-quality assessment in cities with sparse monitoring networks. 
 Reliable estimation of surface-level nitrogen dioxide (NO2) concentrations is critical for air quality assessment in urban regions, where ground-based monitoring networks often provide limited spatial coverage. Satellite observations from Sentinel-5P offer valuable information on tropospheric NO2 columns, but their translation to surface concentrations remains challenging due to strong spatial heterogeneity and meteorological influences. This study investigates the estimation of daily surface-level NO2 concentrations across the Community of Madrid (Spain) by combining Sentinel-5P observations with routinely available ground-based meteorological data using machine learning approaches. Four modelling paradigms were evaluated: Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, hybrid ensemble models based on Random Forest and XGBoost, and Artificial Neural Networks. The analysis systematically assessed the influence of temporal and spatial preprocessing by comparing two temporal aggregation strategies—satellite overpass conditions alone and a dual-time window including antecedent atmospheric conditions—and four spatial configurations ranging from regional aggregation to environmentally coherent clustering. Results indicate that incorporating historical meteorological information through an extended temporal window consistently improves predictive performance across all models. Spatial stratification was found to be equally critical: grouping monitoring stations into environmentally coherent clusters substantially outperformed both purely geometric grids and region-wide aggregation. The best-performing configuration, an Artificial Neural Network combined with simplified coherent spatial clusters, achieved an RMSE of 2.44 μg/m3, an R2 of 0.87, and a MAE of 1.61 μg/m3. These findings demonstrate that high predictive accuracy can be achieved through informed temporal and spatial design choices without increasing model complexity or relying on auxiliary emission inventories or chemical transport models, providing a transferable framework for urban air quality monitoring.

explanation: 该研究使用遥感观测技术（卫星观测）分析了阿拉伯半岛上大气中二氧化碳（CO2）的时空变化，涉及空气质量监测、卫星传感器（GOSAT、OCO-2、OCO-3）和大气组成分析，因此与大气环境遥感技术密切相关。
authors: Chiranjit Das;Ravi Kumar Kunchala;Rama Krishna Karumuri;Harikishan Gandham;Debanjan Paul;Hari Prasad Dasari;Ibrahim Hoteit
title: Unveiling the multiscale variability of atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO<sub>2</sub>) over the Arabian Peninsula using remote sensing observations
abstract:
•This study focused on the multiscale variability of XCO2 over AP using satellite observations during 2010–2023 •Satellite observations shows consistent XCO2trends of 2.35 ppm/yr (GOSAT), 2.36 ppm/yr (OCO-2), and 2.54 ppm/yr (OCO-3) •Urban regions (e.g., Dammam, Kuwait, Dubai) show ∼1 ppm of higher XCO2level relative to regional background. 
 Carbon dioxide (CO2) level over the Arabian Peninsula (AP) is rising due to anthropogenic activities but limited in-situ monitoring sites limits understanding of AP wide CO2 variation. Satellites provide a valuable alternative for monitoring spatiotemporal CO2 variability across AP. This study investigates the multiscale (diurnal, seasonal, interannual, and long-term) variability of the column-averaged dry-air mole fraction of CO2 (XCO2) in the AP from 2010 to 2023 using the Greenhouse Gases Observing Satellite (GOSAT), and the Orbiting Carbon Observatory (OCO-2 and OCO-3) datasets. The number of satellite soundings over the AP increased nearly 100-fold after the launch of OCO-2/3 compared to GOSAT. Specifically, the OCO-3 captured a high density of XCO2 measurements during mid-day hours (7 to 11 UTC), with fewer observations in the morning and late afternoon (3, 14, and 15 UTC). Our results show that XCO2 has a distinct diurnal pattern reflects biospheric activity following solar insolation. XCO2 also shows a clear seasonal cycle, with springtime peaks and summer lows driven by biospheric activity. Annual XCO2 varies in the range of 2.35–2.54 ppm/yr across all satellites, and the influence of the 2015–2016 El Niño event is observed in both GOSAT and OCO-2 datasets. Additionally, analysis of the fossil fuel CO2 footprint reveals urban regions (e.g., Dammam, Riyadh, Jeddah, Dubai) exhibiting an average increase of ∼1 ppm in XCO2 with respect to background concentration, corresponding to a mean fossil CO2 emission of 30 gC/m2/day based on ODIAC emissions.

explanation: 该论文与大气环境遥感技术密切相关，特别是空气质量监测领域。论文探讨了可解释机器学习在空气污染建模中的应用，涉及预测和因果推断任务，这与遥感技术在空气质量监测中的应用高度相关。
authors: Yanchuan Shao;Riyang Liu;Shenyuan Huang;Wen Fang;Jianxun Yang;Miaomiao Liu;Jun Bi;Zongwei Ma
title: Interpretable machine learning for ambient air pollution modeling: A systemic review
abstract:
•The concepts, methods, and applications of interpretable machine learning are summarized in air pollution studies. •Interpretable machine learning could be used for both prediction and causal inference tasks. •The importance of defining the interpretability scope and enhancing the robustness is emphasized. 
 Machine learning (ML) has been extensively applied in air pollution modeling. It provides spatiotemporal predictions with high accuracy compared to traditional approaches. However, the inner mechanism of ML is difficult to interpret due to its black-box nature. The advent of interpretable machine learning (IML) methods could enhance the understanding of variable relationships in ML. Though IML has been introduced in some recent reviews, the concept and technical details have not been fully explored compared to other interpretable methods in related studies. This gap in understanding may hinder the effective and appropriate application of IML in air pollution modeling, underscoring the need for a comprehensive examination of its capabilities and limitations in this context. In this review, we present an overview of the concepts, methods and applications of IML methods. We highlight the difference in explanation frameworks between chemical transport models, statistical models and ML in air pollution studies. The IML methods expand the applicability of ML models, allowing them to be used for both prediction and causal inference tasks. We argue that related studies should clarify the interpretation scope and robustness of variable relationships when employing IML methods in air pollution modeling. Further efforts are required to improve the reliability and scientific consistency of interpretation outcomes derived from IML methods. This review provides a basis for the methodological advancement of interpretable air pollution modeling.
